title: This NotebookLM feature cuts my workload by 50%
summary: Jeff Su demonstrates NotebookLM's mind map feature, which generates visual topic hierarchies from uploaded documents. Users can selectively explore branches of interest rather than reviewing all source material, reducing preparation workload for content projects.
writeup: A 30-second demonstration of NotebookLM's mind map feature as a pre-production research filter. The workflow: load all candidate sources, generate a mind map for an instant visual overview of topics and subtopics, then cherry-pick only the threads worth pursuing. Clicking a subtopic node opens a focused chat directly, collapsing the gap between high-level survey and deep-dive conversation into a single click.
key_points: NotebookLM's mind map renders every topic and subtopic from a source set visually, making it faster to identify which threads are worth exploring than reading through each source individually.

The practical filtering criterion is audience fit: in the worked example, '11 practical techniques' was flagged as worth exploring further while 'agentic toggles API tips' was skipped as too technical for the intended audience.
Clicking a mind map node transitions directly into a chat scoped to that subtopic, so the mind map acts as both a survey instrument and a navigation layer into targeted Q&A.
